code_dossier.name: TraceIdFilter.java
code_dossier.description: A Spring Web Filter component that extracts trace ID from HTTP request headers and propagates it through the request lifecycle via SLF4J MDC (Mapped Diagnostic Context) for distributed tracing and log correlation purposes.

data.detailed_description:
TraceIdFilter is a critical middleware component in the account-provider microservice that implements distributed tracing capability. It extends Spring's OncePerRequestFilter to ensure the filter logic executes exactly once per request, even in complex dispatch scenarios.

The filter intercepts incoming HTTP requests and extracts the 'X-Trace-Id' header value, which is typically propagated from upstream services or API gateways in a microservices architecture. When present, this trace ID is stored in SLF4J's MDC (Mapped Diagnostic Context), making it available to all logging statements within the same thread context throughout the request processing lifecycle.

The implementation follows a proper try-finally pattern to ensure MDC cleanup after request processing, preventing memory leaks and trace ID contamination between requests in thread-pooled environments. This is essential for maintaining accurate distributed tracing across the system.

In the system architecture, this filter serves as the entry point for trace correlation, enabling operations teams to track requests as they flow through multiple services, facilitating debugging, performance monitoring, and issue diagnosis in distributed systems.
responsibilities: Extract X-Trace-Id header from incoming HTTP requests for distributed tracing
Store trace ID in SLF4J MDC for thread-local log correlation
Ensure proper MDC cleanup after request completion to prevent memory leaks
Enable log aggregation and request tracking across microservices
Integrate with Spring's filter chain for seamless request processing

interfaces.name: doFilterInternal

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: The incoming HTTP servlet request containing potential trace headers

parameters.name: filterChain
parameters.description: The filter chain to continue request processing

interfaces.description: Core filter method that processes each HTTP request once, extracting trace ID and managing MDC lifecycle
